judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all three expectations equally well; Report 1 gives a more thorough grounded explanation for the SQL-injection non-issue (citing the enum and Pydantic validation), while Report 2 stays silent on it (also compliant) but adds an unverified, lower-confidence tangent about SQLAlchemy's .all() method.

report_1.evidence: `tenant_id` is accepted as a parameter but never appears in the SQL text or the bound-params dict. Every other read path in this codebase (`list_appointments` → `build_appointment_query` in `db.py`, and `exports.py`'s `nightly_export`) filters explicitly by `Appointment.tenant_id == tenant_id`... `search_appointments` bypasses `build_appointment_query` entirely and writes its own SQL, silently dropping the one scoping mechanism this system has.

report_2.evidence: `tenant_id` is accepted as a function argument but is never referenced in the WHERE clause. Every other read path in this codebase goes through `build_appointment_query()` in `backend/app/db.py`... `search_appointments` bypasses that helper entirely with hand-written SQL and drops the tenant filter.

report_1.evidence: The signature changed from `build_appointment_query(session, tenant_id)` to `build_appointment_query(session, tenant_id, filters)` with **no default value** for `filters`. `exports.py`'s `nightly_export()` — not touched by this PR — still calls `build_appointment_query(session, tenant_id)` with two positional arguments... every call to `nightly_export()`... will raise `TypeError: build_appointment_query() missing 1 required positional argument: 'filters'`

report_2.evidence: `backend/app/exports.py` (completely untouched by this PR... still calls `build_appointment_query(session, tenant_id)` with only two positional arguments at line 9 of `nightly_export()`. This will raise `TypeError: build_appointment_query() missing 1 required positional argument: 'filters'` the next time the nightly per-tenant CSV export runs

report_1.evidence: status is typed `AppointmentStatus` in `api.py`, a closed `str, Enum` (`schemas.py`) with only `booked`/`cancelled`/`completed` as members, and FastAPI/Pydantic rejects any non-member value with a 422 before the handler runs — so there is no live SQL-injection path through the real endpoint today.
